office_does: A state senator writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: A school-board president and small-business owner faces an incumbent senator and former councilmember; both emphasize restraint, public safety, schools, and housing.

candidates.summary: Galbraith is a school-board president, Marine veteran, security specialist, and small-business owner emphasizing taxes, public safety, schools, and parental rights.
highlights: Opposes higher taxes on working families [S1]
Supports law enforcement and parental rights [S1]

taxes.basis: He explicitly opposes higher taxes on working families.

experience.basis: He has several years of elected school-board service but no legislative record.

social.basis: He explicitly supports parental rights in schools.

candidates.summary: Torres is an incumbent senator and former councilmember emphasizing fiscal restraint, public safety, schools, housing, and mental-health services.
highlights: Opposes wasteful spending, unfair taxes, and burdensome regulation [S1]
Prioritizes public safety, schools, housing, and mental-health services [S1]

taxes.basis: She opposes taxes she describes as unfair.

spending.basis: She calls for restrained budgets and opposes waste while also citing major regional investment.

safety.basis: She prioritizes public safety, though the dossier gives no detailed mechanism.

experience.basis: She is an incumbent senator and former city councilmember.
